The Printing Press

Johannes Gutenberg introduced movable-type printing to Europe around 1440 in
Mainz. His most famous work, the Gutenberg Bible, was printed around 1455. The
press used individual metal letters that could be rearranged and reused, making
books far cheaper and faster to produce than hand copying. The technology spread
rapidly and is widely credited with enabling the Reformation and the scientific
revolution by making information widely available.
